title: Cupaniopsis guillauminii
taxonomy: Cupaniopsis guillauminii is classified in the family Sapindaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the soapberry family. Cupaniopsis guillauminii is further classified in the genus Cupaniopsis. The genus Cupaniopsis is a group of flowering plants native to Australia and New Caledonia. Cupaniopsis guillauminii is a species of flowering plant within this genus.
uses: Cupaniopsis guillauminii is believed to have several medicinal uses, including the treatment of various health conditions. Its bark, leaves, and seeds are traditionally used by indigenous communities to make remedies for fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that extracts from the plant may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, Cupaniopsis guillauminii is thought to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cupaniopsis guillauminii for medicinal purposes.

intro: Cupaniopsis guillauminii, also known as the Tamanu nut tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to New Caledonia. The health benefits of Cupaniopsis guillauminii include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and promote tissue repair. The bioactive constituents of this plant include calophyllic acid, a triterpenoid ester responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other triterpenoids and polyphenols. The most common herbal preparations made from Cupaniopsis guillauminii include topical creams and oils, as well as tinctures and capsules, which are used to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
